title: Report on Outstanding Species at Risk Last update June 2010 - Canada.ca
content:
## Report on Outstanding Species at Risk Last update June 2010
Under the Species at Risk Act (SARA), the Governor in Council can, within nine months of receiving an assessment of the status of a species by the Committee on the Status of Endangered Wildlife in Canada (COSEWIC), either;
- accept the assessment and add the species to Schedule 1;
- decide to not add the species to Schedule 1; or
- refer the assessment back to COSEWIC for further information or consideration.
The current status of species under extended consultations or for which a Not List decision was made by the GiC to accommodate further consultations. Decisions to Refer species back to COSEWIC for further consideration are also included.
- Batch 1 - COSEWIC Assessments: May 2002, November 2002, May 2003
- Batch 2 - COSEWIC Assessments: November 2003, May 2004
- Batch 3 - COSEWIC Assessments: November 2004, May 2005
- Batch 4 - COSEWIC Assessments: April 2006
- Batch 5 - COSEWIC Assessments: August 2007
- Batch 6 - COSEWIC Assessments: August 2008
Grizzly Bear ( Ursus arctos ) Northwestern population
Governor in Council Decision: 12 Jan 2005. Not listed at this time, pending completion of a consultation process with the Nunavut Wildlife Management Board. See Rationale for decision Consultations with Nunavut Wildlife Management Board are complete. The Minister of the Environment is awaiting reassessment of this species. COSEWIC is expected to reassess this species on or before May 2012.
Wolverine ( Gulo gulo ) Western population
YT NT NU BC AB SK MB ON
Governor in Council Decision: 12 Jan 2005. Not listed at this time, pending completion of a consultation process with the Nunavut Wildlife Management Board. See Rationale for decision Consultations with Nunavut Wildlife Management Board are complete. The Minister of the Environment is awaiting reassessment of this species. COSEWIC is expected to reassess this species on or before May 2012.
Polar Bear ( Ursus maritimus )
YT NT NU MB ON QC NL Arctic Ocean
Governor in Council Decision: 12 Jan 2005. Not listed at this time, pending completion of a consultation process with the Nunavut Wildlife Management Board. See Rationale for decision Governor in Council Decision: 14 Jul 2005. Referred back to COSEWIC for further information or consideration. See Rationale for decision COSEWIC Reassessment: April 2008, Special Concern. Extended consultations will end on July 4, 2010.
Atlantic Cod ( Gadus morhua ) Arctic population
Governor in Council Decision: 06 Apr 2006. Referred back to COSEWIC for further information or consideration. See Rationale for decision 15 Dec 2006: COSEWIC reaffirmed their original assessment. Minister's Receipt occurred on 15 Dec 2006. April 2010: COSEWIC determined that the Arctic population should be treated as two populations and assessed the Arctic Lakes population as Special Concern and the Arctic Marine population as Data Deficient. The Minister is expected to receive the new assessments in August, 2010.
Bocaccio ( Sebastes paucispinis )
Governor in Council Decision: 06 Apr 2006. Referred back to COSEWIC for further information or consideration. See Rationale for decision 15 Dec 2006: COSEWIC reaffirmed their original assessment. Minister's Receipt occurred on 15 Dec 2006. Consultations ended 30 Dec 2008 and GiC Receipt is targeted for Spring 2010.
Cusk ( Brosme brosme )
Governor in Council Decision: 06 Apr 2006. Referred back to COSEWIC for further information or consideration. See Rationale for decision 15 Dec 2006: COSEWIC reaffirmed their original assessment. Minister's Receipt occurred on 15 Dec 2006. Preliminary consultations ended 30 Dec 2008. Pending further consultations with affected communities.
Lake Winnipeg Physa Snail ( Physa sp.)
Governor in Council Decision: 06 Apr 2006. Referred back to COSEWIC for further information or consideration. See Rationale for decision 15 Dec 2006: COSEWIC reaffirmed their original assessment. Minister's Receipt occurred on 15 Dec 2006. GiC Receipt is targeted for Spring 2010.
Shortjaw Cisco ( Coregonus zenithicus )
Governor in Council Decision: 06 Apr 2006. Referred back to COSEWIC for further information or consideration. See Rationale for decision COSEWIC will proceed with the reassessment once the relevant Aboriginal Traditional Knowledge is available.
Barren-ground Caribou ( Rangifer tarandus groenlandicus ) Dolphin and Union population
Governor in Council Decision: 14 Jul 2005. Not listed at this time, pending completion of a consultation process with the Nunavut Wildlife Management Board. See Rationale for decision The consultation process with the Nunavut Wildlife Management Board has been completed. GiC Receipt is targeted for Spring of 2010.
Peary Caribou ( Rangifer tarandus pearyi )
Governor in Council Decision: 14 Jul 2005. Not listed at this time, pending completion of a consultation process with the Nunavut Wildlife Management Board. Plains Bison ( Bison bison bison )
GiC decision: 14 July 2005. Not listed at this time, for potential economic implications for the Canadian bison industry. See Rationale for decision
Porsild's Bryum ( Mielichhoferia macrocarpa )
Governor in Council Decision: 14 Jul 2005. Not listed at this time, pending completion of a consultation process with the Nunavut Wildlife Management Board. Governor in Council Decision: 15 Aug 2006. The decision was made not to list at that time, pending the harmonization of a process between the Nunavut Land Claims Agreement and SARA. See Rationale for decision Consultations with the Nunavut Wildlife Management Board are currently underway.

NU QC Arctic Ocean Atlantic Ocean

Consultations ended 31 Mar 2006 and GiC Receipt is targeted for Spring 2010.
Bowhead Whale ( Balaena mysticetus ) Hudson Bay-Foxe Basin population
Preliminary consultations ended 31 Mar 2006. This population has since been reassigned by COSEWIC to the Eastern Canada-West Greenland population of Bowhead Whale and re-assessed as Special Concern. Minister's Receipt occurred on 28 Aug 2009. The Eastern Canada-West Greenland population of Bowhead Whale is now undergoing extended consultations. Pending consultations with the Nunavik Marine Region Wildlife Board and the Nunavut Wildlife Management Board.
Bowhead Whale ( Balaena mysticetus ) Davis Strait-Baffin Bay population
Preliminary consultations ended 31 Mar 2006. This population has since been reassigned by COSEWIC to the Eastern Canada-West Greenland population of Bowhead Whale and re-assessed as Special Concern. Minister's Receipt occurred on 28 Aug 2009. The Eastern Canada-West Greenland population of Bowhead Whale is now undergoing extended consultations. Pending consultations with the Nunavik Marine Region Wildlife Board and the Nunavut Wildlife Management Board.
